{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Sakurai, Nara Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanzan Shrine
談山神社 | image = TanzanJinja.JPG | caption = Pagoda of Tanzan Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34|27|57|N|135|51|42|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] | founded_by = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | established = 678 | date_destroyed = | location = 319 Tōnomine, [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture|Nara-ken]]
〒633-0032 | website = {{URL|http://www.tanzan.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanzan Shrine'''|談山神社|Tanzan-jinja|}}, also known as the '''Danzan Shrine''', the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[syncretism]] of [[Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Religious organizations established in the 7th century]] [[category:kk]][[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[category:kk]][[category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 678]] [[category:kk]][[category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]]

The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Religious organizations established in the 7th century]] [[category:kk]][[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[category:kk]][[category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 678]] [[category:kk]][[category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]]

〒633-0032 | website = {{URL|http://www.tanzan.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanzan Shrine'''|談山神社|Tanzan-jinja|}}, also known as the '''Danzan Shrine''', the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[syncretism]] of [[Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[category:kk]][[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:kk]][[category:Religious organizations established in the 7th century]] [[category:kk]][[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[category:kk]][[category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 678]] [[category:kk]][[category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]]

The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[category:Religious organizations established in the 7th century]] [[category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 678]] [[category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]]

the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[Nara Prefecture]], |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[syncretism]] of [[Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Religious organizations established in the 7th century]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 678]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]]

The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Religious organizations established in the 7th century]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 678]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Sakurai, Nara Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanzan Shrine
談山神社 | image = TanzanJinja.JPG | caption = Pagoda of Tanzan Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34|27|57|N|135|51|42|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] | founded_by = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | established = 678 | date_destroyed = | location = 319 Tōnomine, [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture|Nara-ken]]
〒633-0032 | website = {{URL|http://www.tanzan.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanzan Shrine'''|談山神社|Tanzan-jinja|}}, also known as the '''Danzan Shrine''', the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[syncretism]] of [[Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Religious organizations established in the 7th century]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 678]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-27]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]]

Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 〒633-0032 | website = {{URL|http://www.tanzan.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanzan Shrine'''|談山神社|Tanzan-jinja|}}, also known as the '''Danzan Shrine''', the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[syncretism]] of [[Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[syncretism]] of [[Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[syncretism]] of [[Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Sakurai, Nara Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanzan Shrine
談山神社 | image = TanzanJinja.JPG | caption = Pagoda of Tanzan Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34|27|57|N|135|51|42|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] | founded_by = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | established = 678 | date_destroyed = | location = 319 Tōnomine, [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture|Nara-ken]]
〒633-0032 | website = {{URL|http://www.tanzan.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanzan Shrine'''|談山神社|Tanzan-jinja|}}, also known as the '''Danzan Shrine''', the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[syncretism]] of [[Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] century]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 678]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Sakurai, Nara Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanzan Shrine
談山神社 | image = TanzanJinja.JPG | caption = Pagoda of Tanzan Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34|27|57|N|135|51|42|E|region:JP_scale:20000_source:jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] | founded_by = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | established = 678 | date_destroyed = | location = 319 Tōnomine, [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai-shi]], [[Nara Prefecture|Nara-ken]]
〒633-0032 | website = {{URL|http://www.tanzan.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanzan Shrine'''|談山神社|Tanzan-jinja|}}, also known as the '''Danzan Shrine''', the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[syncretism]] of [[Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Religious organizations established in the 7th century]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 678]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Tanzan Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tanzan Shrine]][[Category:dd]] During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[Nara Prefecture]], [[Japan]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[Tendai]] temple built in the [[Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} was the oldest son of [[Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[syncretism]] of [[Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the ranked shrine system after [[World War II]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[Kemari]] [[Matsuri]]'', or [[kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[Kemari]]'' ''[[Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links
