{{ill|Q65257824|WD=Q65257824}}; {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|WD=Q10444949}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanzan Shrine'''|談山神社|Tanzan-jinja|}}, also known as the '''Danzan Shrine''', the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] temple built in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} was the oldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]] of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[:en:Pagoda|Pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]] [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or [[:en:Kickball|Kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[:en:Football|Football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]]'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] }} [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}}

|access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] temple built in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} was the oldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]] of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[:en:Pagoda|Pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]] [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or [[:en:Kickball|Kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[:en:Football|Football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]]'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[:en:Pagoda|Pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]] [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or [[:en:Kickball|Kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[:en:Football|Football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]]'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

|access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] temple built in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} was the oldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]] of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[:en:Pagoda|Pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]] [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or [[:en:Kickball|Kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[:en:Football|Football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]]'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[arz:ضريح تنزان]][[co:commons:Category:Tanzan-jinja]][[de:Tanzan-Schrein]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[fa:معبد تانزان]][[fr:Tanzan-jinja]][[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]][[it:Santuario di Tanzan]][[ja:談山神社]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]][[co:commons:Category:Tanzan-jinja]][[de:Tanzan-Schrein]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[fa:معبد تانزان]][[fr:Tanzan-jinja]][[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]][[it:Santuario di Tanzan]][[ja:談山神社]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]][[sn:commons:Category:Tanzan-jinja]][[de:Tanzan-Schrein]][[en:Tanzan Shrine]][[fa:معبد تانزان]][[fr:Tanzan-jinja]][[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]][[it:Santuario di Tanzan]][[ja:談山神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q488618]] Wikidata name: Tanzan Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Sakurai, Nara Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tanzan Shrine
談山神社 | image = File:Tanzan-jinja, keidai.jpg | caption = Pagoda of Tanzan Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.465806|N|135.861654|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|WD=Q433995}} | founded_by = {{ill|Jōe|WD=Q11452212}} | established = 678 | date_destroyed = | location = 319 Tōnomine, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai-shi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara-ken]]
〒633-0032 | website = {{URL|http://www.tanzan.or.jp/}} | architecture_style ={{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | part_of = {{ill|Q65257824|WD=Q65257824}}; {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|WD=Q10444949}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanzan Shrine'''|談山神社|Tanzan-jinja|}}, also known as the '''Danzan Shrine''', the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] temple built in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} was the oldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]] of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[:en:Pagoda|Pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]] [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or [[:en:Kickball|Kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[:en:Football|Football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]]'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

談山神社 | image = File:Tanzan-jinja, keidai.jpg | caption = Pagoda of Tanzan Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.465806|N|135.861654|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|WD=Q433995}} | founded_by = {{ill|Jōe|WD=Q11452212}} | established = 678 | date_destroyed = | location = 319 Tōnomine, [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai-shi]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara-ken]]
〒633-0032 | website = {{URL|http://www.tanzan.or.jp/}} | architecture_style ={{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | part_of = {{ill|Q65257824|WD=Q65257824}}; {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|WD=Q10444949}} }} {{Nihongo|'''Tanzan Shrine'''|談山神社|Tanzan-jinja|}}, also known as the '''Danzan Shrine''', the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Shrine'''|多武峯社|Tōnomine-sha}} and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a [[:en:Tendai|Tendai]] temple built in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} was the oldest son of [[:en:Fujiwara no Kamatari|Fujiwara no Kamatari]] (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Daigo]] (884 – 930) and [[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Go-Hanazono]] (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of [[:en:Syncretism|Syncretism]] of [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden [[:en:Pagoda|Pagoda]] was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]] [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or [[:en:Kickball|Kickball]] festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of [[:en:Football|Football]] in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''[[:en:Kemari|Kemari]]'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

{{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Daihyakka Zensho (Nipponika) | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | oclc = 153301537 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|WD=Q855964|lt=Tendai}} temple built in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} was the oldest son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|WD=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari}} (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Daigo}} (884 – 930) and {{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Go-Hanazono}} (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of {{ill|Syncretism|en|Syncretism|WD=Q172904|lt=Syncretism}} of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden {{ill|Pagoda|en|Pagoda|WD=Q199451|lt=Pagoda}} was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari}} [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or {{ill|Kickball|en|Kickball|WD=Q1154146|lt=Kickball}} festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of {{ill|Football|en|Football|WD=Q1081491|lt=Football}} in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari}}'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of {{ill|Football|en|Football|WD=Q1081491|lt=Football}} in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari}}'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei | title = 談山神社 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tanzan Shrine | id = dlc 2009238904 | oclc = 173191044 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} It is located 5km from [[Ishibutai Kofun]].{{Cite web |title=Google Maps |url=https://www.google.com/maps/dir/'34.466861,135.826139'/34.465833,135.861667/@34.464802,135.8394253,15.5z/data=!4m7!4m6!1m3!2m2!1d135.826139!2d34.466861!1m0!3e2?hl=en&entry=ttu |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|WD=Q855964|lt=Tendai}} temple built in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} was the oldest son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|WD=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari}} (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Daigo}} (884 – 930) and {{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Go-Hanazono}} (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of {{ill|Syncretism|en|Syncretism|WD=Q172904|lt=Syncretism}} of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden {{ill|Pagoda|en|Pagoda|WD=Q199451|lt=Pagoda}} was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari}} [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or {{ill|Kickball|en|Kickball|WD=Q1154146|lt=Kickball}} festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of {{ill|Football|en|Football|WD=Q1081491|lt=Football}} in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari}}'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} was the oldest son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|WD=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari}} (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Daigo}} (884 – 930) and {{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Go-Hanazono}} (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of {{ill|Syncretism|en|Syncretism|WD=Q172904|lt=Syncretism}} of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden {{ill|Pagoda|en|Pagoda|WD=Q199451|lt=Pagoda}} was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari}} [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or {{ill|Kickball|en|Kickball|WD=Q1154146|lt=Kickball}} festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of {{ill|Football|en|Football|WD=Q1081491|lt=Football}} in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari}}'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The emperors {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Daigo}} (884 – 930) and {{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Go-Hanazono}} (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of {{ill|Syncretism|en|Syncretism|WD=Q172904|lt=Syncretism}} of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism}} and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden {{ill|Pagoda|en|Pagoda|WD=Q199451|lt=Pagoda}} was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari}} [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or {{ill|Kickball|en|Kickball|WD=Q1154146|lt=Kickball}} festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of {{ill|Football|en|Football|WD=Q1081491|lt=Football}} in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari}}'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

and the {{Nihongo|'''Tōnomine Temple'''|多武峯寺|Tōnomine-ji}}, is a [[Shinto]] shrine in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 談山神社 | url = |access-date=2023-10-26 |website=Google Maps |language=en}} ==History== The shrine traces its origin to a {{ill|Tendai|en|Tendai|WD=Q855964|lt=Tendai|1=Tendai}} temple built in the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} (538 – 710) called Tōnomine-ji, built by the monk {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} (643 – 666). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} was the oldest son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|WD=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}} (614 – 669), founder of the [[Fujiwara clan]]. {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} located the temple on [[Tōnomine]], a peak of on the southern side of [[Mount Goharetsu]] ({{Convert|619|m|ft}}). {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} moved the remains of Kamatari to a 13-story pagoda on the site. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Daigo|1=Emperor Daigo}} (884 – 930) and {{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Go-Hanazono|1=Emperor Go-Hanazono}} (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of {{ill|Syncretism|en|Syncretism|WD=Q172904|lt=Syncretism|1=Syncretism}} of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden {{ill|Pagoda|en|Pagoda|WD=Q199451|lt=Pagoda|1=Pagoda}} was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari|1=Kemari}} [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or {{ill|Kickball|en|Kickball|WD=Q1154146|lt=Kickball|1=Kickball}} festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of {{ill|Football|en|Football|WD=Q1081491|lt=Football|1=Football}} in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari|1=Kemari}}'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

on the site. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the temple developed together with the prosperity of the Fujiwara clan. The emperors {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Daigo|1=Emperor Daigo}} (884 – 930) and {{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Go-Hanazono|1=Emperor Go-Hanazono}} (1419 – 1471) attached special reverence to the temple, and bestowed it with various honorifics.{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 定恵 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-05 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = {{ill|Jo'e|ja|定恵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jo'e|1=Jōe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452212}} | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }}{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = 多武峰 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-09-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = Tōnomine | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Under ''[[shinbutsu-shūgō]]'', a system of {{ill|Syncretism|en|Syncretism|WD=Q172904|lt=Syncretism|1=Syncretism}} of {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|WD=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}} and ''[[kami]]'' worship, the site was both a Shinto shrine and a Buddhist temple. The Tanzan Shrine and Tōnomine-ji coexisted on the same site. Tōnomine-ji had two subtemples located within its precincts, Myōraku-ji (妙楽寺) and {{Nihongo|Shōryō-in|聖霊院||}}. The shrine received significant financial support from the [[:en:Tokugawa bakufu|Tokugawa bakufu]] during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} (1603 – 1868). During the anti-Buddhist ''[[shinbutsu bunri]]'' movement after the [[Meiji Restoration]] of 1868 Tanzan Shrine was designated solely as a Shinto shrine dedicated to the worship of the ''kami'' of Fujiwara no Kamatari. The Buddhist structures of the shrine were rededicated as Shinto structures. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], the Tanzan Shrine was designated a ''bekkaku kanreisha'' in 1874, an Imperial shrine of special status. The shrine lost this designation after the abolition of the Ranked shrine system after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. ==Structures== The present thirteen-story wooden {{ill|Pagoda|en|Pagoda|WD=Q199451|lt=Pagoda|1=Pagoda}} was built in 1532, and is a reconstruction of the structure built by Jo'e in the Asuka Period. The pagoda is designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. The ''[[honden]]'', or main hall, is built in the ''[[Kasuga-zukuri]]'' style. It is dedicated to Fujiwara no Kamatari. ==Kemari Matsuri== A ''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari|1=Kemari}} [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'', or {{ill|Kickball|en|Kickball|WD=Q1154146|lt=Kickball|1=Kickball}} festival, is held every year on April 29 and the second Sunday in November. On this day, people in ancient costumes and arranged in a circle play a form of {{ill|Football|en|Football|WD=Q1081491|lt=Football|1=Football}} in which they kick a ball made of deerskin to each other. == See also == * For an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Buddhism, Japanese Buddhist art, and Japanese Buddhist temple architecture, see the [[Glossary of Japanese Buddhism]]. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} ==Gallery== image:Tanzan Shrine 3.jpg image:Tanzan jinja04s3200.jpg|Thirteen-storied pagoda image:Tanzan jinja pagoda from below.jpg image:Tanzan jinja lanterns at balcony.jpg|Lanterns image:Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|A secondary building at the shrine. image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 1.jpg|''{{ill|Kemari|en|Kemari|WD=Q12224003|lt=Kemari|1=Kemari}}'' ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' at the shrine image:Kemari Matsuri at Tanzan Shrine 2.jpg|''Kemari Matsuri'' at the shrine == External links == {{Commons Category|Tanzan-jinja}} *[http://www.tanzan.or.jp/ Official Site (Japanese)] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Tanzan Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح تنزان]] {{Commons category|Tanzan-jinja}} [[de:Tanzan-Schrein]] [[fa:معبد تانزان]] [[fr:Tanzan-jinja]] [[hy:Տանզանի տաճար]] [[it:Santuario di Tanzan]] [[ja:談山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q488618}} [[Category:7th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]
